title: Phone Keypad Letter Combinations
description:
Given a string of digits 2..9, return all possible letter combinations that the number could represent using the telephone keypad mapping. Use backtracking to append letters for each digit in order.

Constraints:
- 0 ≤ |digits| ≤ 10
explanation:
Intuition
The branching factor per digit is three or four. Build strings by choosing each letter for the current digit and recursing to the next.

Approach
1. Initial Setup: Map digits to strings
2. Main Algorithm: DFS index i; for each letter in map[digits[i]] append and recurse
3. Return Result: Add built string at end

Complexity Analysis
- Time Complexity: Product of choices
- Space Complexity: O(length of output string) recursion

Key Insights
- Return [] when digits empty
- Precompute mapping for clarity

Common Pitfalls
- Concatenating in place can be messy; use path string
- Forgetting to advance index
